title: Section 24

source.title: NPNF2-03. Theodoret, Jerome, Gennadius, & Rufinus: Historical Writings

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: It is written that in our Lord’s passion there was darkness over the earth from the sixth hour until the ninth.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: To this also you will find the Prophet witnessing, “Thy Sun shall go down at mid-day.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: And again, the Prophet Zechariah, “In that day there shall be no more light.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: There shall be cold and frost in one day, and that day known to the Lord; and it shall be neither day nor night, but at evening time there shall be light.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: What plainer language could the Prophet have used for his words to seem not so much a prophecy of the future as a narrative of the past?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: He foretold both the cold and the frost.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: For Peter was warming himself at the fire because it was cold: and he was suffering cold not only in respect of the time (the early hour), but also of his faith.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: There is added, “and that day shall be known to the Lord; and it shall be neither day nor night.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: Did he not plainly speak of the darkness interposed in the day, and then the light afterwards restored?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: That was not day, for it did not begin with sun-rise, neither was it complete night, for it did not, when the day was ended, receive its due space from the beginning or prolong it to the end; but the light which had been driven away by the crime of wicked men is restored at evening time.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: For after the ninth hour, the darkness is driven away, and the sun is restored to the world.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Apostles' Creed.
verses.text: Again, another Prophet witnesses of the same, “The light shall be darkened upon the earth in the day-time.”
